items.title: Salesman jailed 10 years, caned for causing grievous hurt to motorcyclist with cleaver on Federal Highway
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 8 — A salesman was sentenced to 10 years’ imprisonment and two strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after he pleaded guilty to causing injuries to a motorcyclist on the Federal Highway last month. The accused was charged with voluntarily causing grievous hurt to the motorcyclist using a meat cleaver, a weapon capable of causing death, at KM14.6 of the motorcycle lane along the Federal Highway near Section 13 at about 8.19 am on June 11. The victim suffered serious injuries, including fractures to his shoulder, left arm, fingers and wrist, as well as injuries to his head and left arm.

items.title: Rescuers search for missing in China storms after 100,000 flee
items.summary: LIULAN: Rescuers scoured flooded parts of China for survivors on Wednesday after storms killed 17 people and caused dozens of rivers to overflow and a reservoir dam to burst, with officials warning of more rain. In the southern region of Guangxi, six people died and at least 130,000 people were moved to safety after torrential rain and severe flooding from Typhoon Maysak, regional officials said, warning rain would continue to fall there and in neighbouring Guangdong province on Wednesday. Fast-flowing muddy water burst the banks of 40 rivers and waterways in Guangxi, damaging nearly 13,000 acres of agricultural land, state media reported.

items.title: Iran targets sites in Bahrain, Kuwait after wave of US strikes
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait on Wednesday after the US launched a wave of military strikes on Iran in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. There was no immediate comment on the strikes from the US military. The US earlier unleashed fresh military strikes and revoked a licence allowing Iran to sell oil in response to attacks on three tankers in the strait.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan’s Muhd Najib vows to develop Bandar Universiti Pagoh into a new socio‑economic hub
items.summary: PAGOH, July 8 — Bandar Universiti Pagoh should be further developed with more sustainable urban facilities to transform it into a new engine of socio-economic growth, particularly for residents of the Bukit Pasir state constituency. To that end, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Muhd Najib Lep is committed to enhancing the education hub if elected in the upcoming Johor state election, saying it is crucial to ensure the economic benefits are felt directly by villagers and small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Muhd Najib, who is also secretary of the Pagoh division of Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah), said the full potential of Bandar Universiti Pagoh, which is home to four higher education institutions, including the Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia (UTHM) Pagoh campus and the Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) Pagoh campus, should be fully harnessed.

items.title: OpenAI to unveil GPT-5.6 on Thursday after delaying launch
items.summary: OpenAI will publicly launch its most capable model, GPT-5.6, on Thursday, after delaying the launch last month on the US government's request amid national security concerns that powerful AI systems could be misused. This comes on the heels of the US government lifting curbs on Anthropic's latest Fable and Mythos AI models last week, less than three weeks after the company was ordered to suspend their access over national security risks. Axios, which broke the news on the OpenAI launch, reported citing a source familiar with the matter, that the US Department of Commerce had approved a broad launch of GPT-5.6, following additional government testing under Washington's new oversight framework for frontier AI.

items.title: A1 AK Koh to buy RM16.7mil land in Puchong Jaya for regional expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ACE Market-listed A1 AK Koh Group Bhd has proposed to acquire a 99-year leasehold parcel of land in Puchong Jaya, Selangor, for RM16.73 million to develop a new regional sales and marketing office. In a circular to shareholders filed on Bursa Malaysia, the company said its subsidiary AK Koh Enterprise Sdn Bhd, will acquire the 4,089.85 square metres vacant commercial land from Qualitypack Properties Sdn Bhd. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Myanmar man jailed for misappropriating nearly 6,000 litres of diesel
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A Myanmar national was sentenced to six months' jail by the Sessions Court here after pleading guilty to misappropriating 5,808 litres of controlled diesel without a valid licence. The charge was framed under Section 20(1) of the Control of Supplies Act, which makes it illegal to move, remove or transfer controlled and subsidised goods from licensed premises without written permission from the Supply Controller. Deputy public prosecutor Preeya Darrsini Sugumaran prosecuted, assisted by prosecuting officer Mohd Hilmi Abu Hassan.

items.title: Over 75,000 crypto mining machines seized in nationwide raids since 2022, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — More than 75,000 cryptocurrency mining machines have been seized in over 3,000 raids nationwide between 2022 and May this year, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar said. He said 629 people were arrested during coordinated operations by the Royal Malaysia Police, Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), local authorities and other relevant agencies. Shamsul Anuar said the illegal activity continues due to strong demand for digital assets and the potential for significant profits from their price fluctuations.

items.title: Johor election: BN targets fence-sitters in final campaign push
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional will focus on winning over fence-sitters during the final week of campaigning for the Johor election. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the strategy would be the coalition's main priority as it seeks to engage every voter before polling day on Saturday. Asyraf Wajdi said all BN election machinery had also been reminded to conduct their campaigns in a civil and respectful manner by refraining from abusive language and the spread of slander.

items.title: Pekat Group secures two data centre infrastructure jobs worth RM46.9mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pekat Group Bhd has secured two contracts totalling RM46.94 million to supply and install earthing and lightning protection systems for data centre developments in Negri Sembilan and Johor. The second contract, worth RM27.21 million, involves the supply and installation of earthing and lightning protection systems for a data centre in Johor, with completion targeted by Sept 19, 2027. Pekat group chief executive officer Tai Yee Chee said the latest contract underscores clients' confidence in Pekat's technical expertise and execution capabilities.

items.title: Workshop owner pleads not guilty to causing grievous hurt to man with machete in Puchong
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 8 — A motor workshop owner pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of causing grievous hurt to a man by hacking him with a machete at a premises in Puchong last year. K. Devaidran, 42, entered the plea after the charge was read out in Tamil before Judge ‘Ainul Bashiirah Dona Don Biyajid. The court also set Aug 13 for mention.

items.title: World Cup screening ends in brawl at Johor restaurant
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A brawl involving a group of football fans watching the FIFA World Cup at a nasi kandar restaurant in Taman Universiti here early today has gone viral on social media. A restaurant employee, who declined to be named, said the incident was the first of its kind in the 10 years the eatery has been operating. The employee said the situation was eventually brought under control and did not escalate into a more serious incident.

items.title: Malaysia urges Islamic world to draw lessons from Gaza tragedy, shape future of humanity, says religious affairs minister
items.summary: The tragedy has also signalled for a renewal of the Islamic civilisation to one that is capable of contributing meaningfully to global peace and ethical leadership, he said, during a plenary session at the International Islamic Civilisation Forum in Tashkent, Uzbekistan. The Muslim world must look beyond its glorious past, Zulkifli said. “The question before us is therefore not whether Islamic civilisation once attained greatness.

items.title: Penang police to dispose of RM1.1mil in obscene film VCDs, DVDs
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police will dispose of VCDs and DVDs containing obscene films worth an estimated RM1.1 million at the northeast district police headquarters this Friday. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said the items formed the bulk of seized goods worth RM1.28 million linked to 565 cases. He said the seized items were from probes into contraband, vice and gambling cases between 2010 and 2017.

items.title: Johor election: 'Onn tak Onn' phenomenon reflects growing acceptance of Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The 'Onn tak Onn' phenomenon seen throughout the Johor election campaign reflects the public's growing acceptance of Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's leadership as Johor Menteri Besar, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. He said the trend also signalled that voters were familiar with Onn Hafiz as a leader and recognised that Barisan Nasional candidates were not merely making promises during the campaign. "Our campaign is simple.

items.title: AirBorneo to rationalise rural routes as it strengthens reliability
items.summary: KUCHING: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd will rationalise selected rural air services (RAS) frequencies as part of efforts to improve reliability and aircraft serviceability, chief executive officer Megat Ardian Aminuddin said. AirBorneo currently operates 14 dedicated turboprop aircraft for RAS, comprising eight ATR 72-500 aircraft and six Twin Otters. Megat Ardian said high-demand frequencies would be maintained.

items.title: RM375 Kuching-KL fare to stabilise market, says AirBorneo
items.summary: KUCHING: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) says its RM375 all-in one-way economy class fare for Kuching-Kuala Lumpur route is aimed at stabilising airfares between Sarawak and Peninsular Malaysia as the state-owned airline prepares to begin jet operations this month. AirBorneo chief executive officer (CEO) Megat Ardian Aminuddin said the fare was introduced after the airline benchmarked prices offered by other carriers on the route, including average lower fares recorded over the past six months and last year. He said the RM375 fare included airport taxes, meals, baggage and seat selection.

items.title: Workshop owner charged over parang attack
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A workshop owner pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to a charge of attacking a man with a parang last year. Deputy public prosecutor Amir Saifullah Mohammad Sallehuddin asked the court to set bail at RM20,000 with one surety. After hearing both parties, 'Ainul Bashiirah Dona allowed bail of RM10,000 with one surety and allowed the prosecution's additional conditions.

items.title: Malaysia’s international arrivals rise 3.4pc to 17.5 million despite global challenges, says Tourism Malaysia
items.summary: Tourism Malaysia director-general Mohd Amirul Rizal Abdul Rahim said the growth was supported by visitors from Asean, China, Japan, Australia, Taiwan and Hong Kong, as well as several Central Asian countries, including Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan. “Nevertheless, the 3.4 per cent increase shows that Malaysia’s tourism industry remains resilient,” he told reporters after the signing of a Memorandum of Collaboration (MoC) between Tourism Malaysia and Tripadvisor here today. Under the two-year collaboration, which runs until the end of 2027, Tourism Malaysia will gain access to Tripadvisor’s performance data and travel insights to strengthen its data-driven tourism planning and marketing strategies, while also exploring co-branding campaigns, digital innovation and programmes recognising excellence in Malaysia’s tourism industry.

items.title: Disabled senior citizen dies in Kuantan house fire
items.summary: KUANTAN: A senior citizen with a disability (PwD) is believed to have died from smoke inhalation after a fire broke out at his double-storey house in Taman Seri Mahkota Jaya here today. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim said the Taman Tas Fire and Rescue Station received an emergency call about the incident at 9.30am before dispatching eight firefighters to the scene. The house was filled with thick smoke.

items.title: PAC demands strict oversight, financial discipline for RM11.22bil LCS project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has urged the government to enforce strict monitoring and financial discipline on the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project to ensure all five vessels are delivered within the capped budget of RM11.22 billion. "Mindef and the Finance Ministry must maintain strict financial discipline to ensure the total contract cost of the LCS project remains below the final ceiling of RM11.22 billion. "Mindef must ensure that this project is successfully completed and all five ships delivered to the RMN in accordance with the established timeline and costs," PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said.

items.title: MATTA urges review of diesel subsidy exclusion for tourism transport operators
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents (MATTA) has urged the government to reconsider its decision to exclude licensed tourism transport operators from the diesel subsidy programme, warning that the move could affect Malaysia's tourism industry and travellers. MATTA president Nigel Wong disputed the recent characterisation by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan that diesel subsidies for tourism transport operators would only benefit foreign tourists, saying licensed tourism vehicles serve both domestic and international travellers. "It is therefore incorrect to assume that diesel subsidies for tourism transport operators would benefit only foreign tourists.

items.title: Malaysia aims to be leading regional maritime hub with Port Klang’s Third Terminal, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Malaysia aims to become a leading maritime hub in South-east Asia through the development of Port Klang’s Third Terminal on Carey Island, which is expected to take nearly 20 years to be fully completed, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said Port Klang currently has a handling capacity of about 15.14 million twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs), compared with Singapore’s Tuas Port, which is capable of handling up to 60 million TEUs annually. “Tuas Port is three times larger than the existing Port Klang.

items.title: Seremban police: Several major roads to close for post‑state polls simulation tomorrow and Friday
items.summary: He said among the main roads affected are Jalan Yam Tuan, Jalan Dato’ Sheikh Ahmad, Lorong Khalsa and Jalan Lim Kee Siong. “The affected junctions and roads to be closed or diverted are Jalan Tuanku Hassan/Jalan Yam Tuan (Madura), Jalan Lee Fong Yee/Jalan Yam Tuan, Jalan Lim Kee Siong/Jalan Jawa, and Lorong Khalsa/Lorong Jawa,” he said in a statement here today. Mohd Yatim said motorists are advised to use alternative routes, including Jalan Dato’ Abdul Malik, Jalan Tuanku Hassan, Jalan Tuanku Munawir and Jalan Dato’ Abdul Kadir.

items.title: Private land used for key Sentul road being acquired, says KL mayor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A privately owned plot of land through which a key road in Sentul passes is being acquired, with the process expected to be completed within four months, said Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud. Commenting on a road closure notice affecting Jalan Taman Datuk Senu and parts of Jalan Taman Datuk Senu 3, Fadlun said Kuala Lumpur City Hall was in the midst of acquiring the land. Fadlun said the process, which involves DBKL working with the Lands and Mines Department, was already at the approval stage.

items.title: Mindef: LCS project reaches 78.1pct completion as of May 31
items.summary: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) said the project, which involves the construction of naval assets for the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), had incurred total expenditure of RM8.457 billion, or 76.2 per cent of the project's overall value. "The Norwegian government's decision to revoke the export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system to Malaysia will not affect the delivery schedule of the LCS vessels, as guaranteed by Lumut Naval Shipyard (Lunas) to the Defence Ministry," the ministry said in a written parliamentary reply published on the parliament website. Mindef said the LCS would retain its key capabilities, including anti-submarine warfare, anti-air warfare, electronic warfare, and a Combat Management System (CMS) that integrates these capabilities with the ship's radar and sensor systems.

items.title: Cloud‑based system speeds up patient flow at public clinics, 81pc seen within 60 minutes, says deputy health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR July, 8 — The implementation of the Cloud-Based Clinical Management System (CCMS) at government health clinics has helped reduce patient waiting times, with 81 per cent of patients now able to see medical officers within 60 minutes. Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said the adoption of the Dental Information System (DIS) at dental clinics and the District Hospital Information System (DHIS) at hospitals had also improved service efficiency while helping to address long waiting times and congestion at public healthcare facilities. Hanifah Hajar said MOH would continue expanding healthcare digitalisation, with plans to implement CCMS at 2,917 health clinics and DIS at 728 dental clinics nationwide by 2028.

items.title: Iraq's holy cities host funeral processions for Khamenei
items.summary: NAJAF: Crowds thronged the streets of Najaf on Wednesday as the coffin of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei moved through the holy city in a procession devoted to Iraq, home to Shia Islam's most sacred shrines. Iran began six days of public funeral ceremonies for Khamenei on Saturday, including a dedicated day to neighbouring Iraq, a Shia powerhouse with close ties to Tehran. After a massive procession in Iran's holy city of Qom, Iraqi officials and senior politicians received the remains of Khamenei on Tuesday night at Najaf international airport in the presence of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and one of the late leader's sons.

items.title: KKIA expansion awaits resolution of land issues, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The nearly RM500 million expansion project for Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) is expected to begin once land and site-related issues are resolved with the Sabah government, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said. "As for the Tawau Airport expansion, works are already underway. However, we are still awaiting the Sabah government's decision on details concerning the project site," he said.

items.title: Vietnam looks at more coal power plants as Iran war complicates LNG plans
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam's government is considering building more coal-fired power plants to ensure it can meet its energy needs, the government said on Wednesday, as the Iran war further complicates its slow development of LNG facilities. "In recent times, the unfolding conflicts in the Middle East have impacted the security of LNG supplies, creating a need to strengthen energy security," the government said in a statement on Wednesday. It said it may adjust its national power plan to add more coal-generated energy to the mix.

items.title: Tiong urges collaboration to boost handicrafts overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: State governments and private companies have been urged to collaborate with the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry to promote local products, including batik, in international markets. Its minister, Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing, said such cooperation was important to give local products greater international exposure. Tiong said Kraftangan Malaysia currently provided extensive support to entrepreneurs in marketing local products through both physical and digital channels.

items.title: Israel must immediately release detained Gaza hospital chief: UN experts
items.summary: GENEVA: United Nations experts demanded Tuesday that Israel immediately release a Gaza hospital director who has been held since December 2024 and who reportedly has suffered severe torture and life-threatening injuries. "Dr. Abu Safiya's ongoing arbitrary detention without charges or trial is a reflection of Israel's systematic targeting of Palestinian health workers," four independent UN experts said in a statement. The experts also included the special rapporteur on the rights situation in the Palestinian territories Francesca Albanese, who has faced harsh Israel criticism and allegations of anti-Semitism from Israel amid her repeated accusations that Israel is committing "genocide" in Gaza.

items.title: Rohingya man held over alleged rape of teenager in Kelantan
items.summary: BACHOK: Police have arrested a 32-year-old Rohingya man suspected of raping a 19-year-old woman in Kampung Kuar, near Tawang, last month. Police obtained a six-day remand order against him today from the Bachok magistrate's court under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code. Bachok police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin confirmed the arrest when contacted.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia turns marginally lower at midday on mild profit-taking
items.summary: Among active stocks, Zetrix AI remained unchanged at 75.5 sen, GIIB and Pentech eased half a sen each to 49.5 sen and 27.5 sen, respectively, and AirAsia X lost four sen to RM1.14, while Dagang NeXchange added one sen to 44 sen. Among the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 48 sen to RM34.22, Allianz Malaysia gained 28 sen to RM20.88, Kuala Lumpur Kepong advanced 20 sen to RM21.88, Malaysian Pacific Industries climbed 16 sen to RM46.00, while United Malacca was 13 sen higher at RM6.07. Fraser & Neave led the losers, falling 32 sen to RM29.34, Nestle dipped 28 sen to RM90.52, Petronas Gas lost 18 sen to RM17.62, Press Metal Aluminium shed 17 sen to RM7.50, and Hong Leong Industries slipped 12 sen to RM18.82.

items.title: France's Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027, appeal her conviction
items.summary: PARIS: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced on Tuesday that she will run for president in 2027, after an appeals court shortened her ban on running for office, even as it upheld her conviction for embezzling EU funds to pay party staff. She also said she would appeal the conviction. "Tonight, I am a candidate in the presidential election," Le Pen said in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV, hours after the ruling.

items.title: DPM Zahid: 12 million Malaysians have signed up for MyDigital ID as of June 30, with 114 govt services integrated
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the government is continuing to expand the use of MyDigital ID as a single sign-on mechanism for online government services through an integration strategy implemented at both the federal and state levels. He said the Chief Secretary to the Government issued a directive on Jan 14, 2025, requiring all ministries, federal departments, statutory bodies, state secretaries and local authorities to implement the single sign-on system using MyDigital ID. At the state government level, he said 19 online applications have so far been integrated with MyDigital ID, while another 28 applications remain under development.

items.title: GB Bond gets Bursa's nod to list on ACE Market in Q3
items.summary: The initial public offering (IPO) comprises 64.3 million new ordinary shares and an offer for sale of 42.88 million existing ordinary shares. Upon completion of the exercise, GB Bond's enlarged issued share capital will stand at 412.3 million shares. "The planned new factory is expected to support the company's manufacturing capacity expansion for industrial adhesives and sealants.

items.title: S&P Dow Jones puts Indonesia on watch list for market downgrade
items.summary: SINGAPORE: S&P Dow Jones Indices warned Indonesia risks a downgrade to frontier from emerging status due to market transparency issues, echoing caution from rival index provider MSCI in the latest setback for the country. Stocks in Jakarta, the worst-performing major stock market this year, fell 1% amid broader risk-off sentiment on Wednesday. S&P DJI said it was continuing to monitor efforts to improve stock ownership transparency, including exchange-led reforms aimed at addressing disclosure concerns and the potential impact on market liquidity.

items.title: Loke: Carey Island port to be developed in phases over 20 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed Carey Island port project, aimed at transforming Port Klang into a regional megaport, could take up to 20 years to complete as it will be developed in phases, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. He said the project's long timeline reflected its scale, with planning dating back several years. "Completion will take years.

items.title: Eraman bets on upgraded KLIA Terminal 2 duty-free mall to lift travel retail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports (Niaga) Sdn Bhd's duty-free arm, Eraman, has reopened its upgraded duty-free mall at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2. The refurbished mall is part of efforts to position KLIA as a regional travel retail hub by offering a wider product range, enhanced passenger experience and more immersive shopping concepts. Spanning 1,194 square metres, the upgraded mall features a redesigned layout aimed at improving passenger flow and creating a more seamless shopping experience for travellers departing through the international gates.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan: LPT3 to be developed under public-private partnership model, bidder to bear full cost
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The proposed East Coast Expressway 3 (LPT3) project will be implemented through a public-private partnership (PPP) model, with the successful bidder expected to bear the full cost of development, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said today. However, he said the highway’s toll rates have yet to be finalised. He said the proposed 122km highway would be developed under a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) concession model, although no concessionaire has been appointed.

items.title: Mohd Uzir leaves behind modernised, more integrated national statistics system, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 8 — The 36-year service of Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin in Malaysia’s statistics system came to an end with a lasting legacy, having strengthened the country’s statistical framework through the National Statistical System Strengthening (PSSN) agenda. The Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), in a statement today, said the agenda had driven several strategic reforms, including the establishment of the National Statistics and Data Council (MSDN) at state and district levels, the modernisation of the Statistics Act 1965 (Act 415) to meet digital-era needs, and capacity building in artificial intelligence (AI) and data science. “Mohd Uzir also pioneered the data democratisation agenda through OpenDOSM NextGen, expanding public access to official statistics through an open and user-friendly platform, while enhancing digital initiatives such as MyPAKW and MyLabourHub to ensure statistical services remain relevant,” DOSM said.

items.title: Johor polls: Malaysian border control agency activates maximum‑capacity operations at BSI, KSAB for returning voters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) will operate at maximum capacity at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) to facilitate the return of voters from Singapore for the 16th Johor state election this Saturday (July 11). Its director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said the initiative includes opening dedicated lanes, activating hybrid counters and implementing contra-flow lanes to ensure smooth traffic flow and traveller movement at the country’s two main land entry points. “At BSI, the measures include opening dedicated lanes, with 38 inbound counters at the car zone, as well as the full operation of 35 e-gates, two quick response (QR) code counters and 18 manual counters throughout the period.

items.title: Johor polls: Over 300,000 voters expected to travel home, heavier traffic anticipated, says LLM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — More than 300,000 voters are expected to travel home to vote, leading to increased traffic on highways, especially in Johor, ahead of Saturday’s 16th Johor state election, said the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM). The public can check the latest traffic updates and highway incidents via LLM’s official Facebook page and TikTok account @llmtrafik, or contact the LLM Traffic Management Centre at 1-800-88-7752. Live highway traffic updates via CCTV cameras are also available on LLM’s official website, www.llm.gov.my, and the LLM Info Trafik WhatsApp channel.

items.title: LCS delivery schedule on track despite Norway’s missile export ban
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The Norwegian government’s cancellation of its export licence for the NSM (Naval Strike Missile) system to Malaysia will not affect the delivery schedule of the country’s littoral combat ships (LCS), Parliament was told. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin said in a written reply dated July 7 that the scheduled delivery of the ships is guaranteed by the Lumut Naval Shipyard (LUNAS). The Ministry of Defence has spent RM8.45 billion on the LCS project as of May 31 this year, representing 76.2 per cent of the overall project value, Parliament was told.

items.title: Future of finance hinges on human‑AI balance anchored in ethics, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes more embedded in finance, the future of the industry will need to combine human judgment with machine intelligence, guided by ethical leadership, accountability and a culture of responsible innovation. Minister of Finance II, Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, said this in his special address at the Asian Institute of Chartered Bankers (AICB) Nexus 2026 Conference, here today. Amir Hamzah said a resilient banking system depends not only on sound regulation, capital strength and technological capability, but also on people.

items.title: Speaker urges MPs to undergo regular health screenings as Kuala Terengganu MP is hospitalised at IJN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul today reminded Members of Parliament to undergo regular health screenings after an MP was hospitalised at the National Heart Institute (IJN). Johari said the annual health screening programme for MPs would continue this year in collaboration with the Ministry of Health (MOH), after being implemented since 2023. “These health screenings are invaluable in detecting any health issues affecting Honourable Members at an early stage, enabling immediate preventive and proactive measures through lifestyle modifications, medication or appropriate early medical intervention,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia targets 30,000 EV charging points by 2030, says Miti
items.summary: “The government is working with stakeholders, particularly TNB, to provide sufficient substations to support charging points, as charging facilities require adequate electricity supply. “The government is also discussing incentives for Charging Point Operators (CPOs) to encourage them to build more charging stations. Without sufficient substations, charging points cannot be developed,” he said.

items.title: Putrajaya open to RCI on ‘corporate mafia’ claims after investigations, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The government is open to considering a Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) into allegations of a so-called “corporate mafia” involving claims of collusion with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), including those linked to former MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, Parliament was told. Law Minister Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said in a written parliamentary reply issued yesterday that the government had not ruled out calls for an RCI, but any decision would have to follow the proper procedures and take into account the outcome of ongoing police investigations. The allegations of a “corporate mafia” first emerged from claims made on a whistleblower blog before being reported by Bloomberg, which published two investigative reports citing MACC insiders among others.

items.title: Miti: EV import policy revised after tax incentives end, RM200,000 CIF threshold set
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The government has revised its electric vehicle (EV) import policy following the expiry of tax incentives for imported Completely Built-Up (CBU) EVs, which were introduced from 2022 until Dec 31, 2025 to encourage early adoption and support the long-term development of Malaysia’s automotive ecosystem. He was responding to Julau MP Datuk Larry Soon @ Larry Sng Wei Shien, who asked whether the government had conducted an impact assessment on recent policy decisions that resulted in higher prices for imported EVs, particularly in terms of consumer affordability, EV adoption targets, investor confidence and Malaysia’s competitiveness as a regional EV hub. Sim said Malaysia’s EV development strategy consists of three phases, beginning with the adoption phase from 2022 until the end of 2025, when incentives were provided to encourage Malaysians to adopt EVs and support the development of the EV ecosystem.

items.title: Muhyiddin says Perikatan may not form Johor govt, hopes to win enough seats for checks and balances
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 8 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) may not be able to form the Johor government after the state election, but the coalition hopes to win enough seats to provide effective checks and balances. According to a Free Malaysia Today (FMT) report, PN chairman Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin acknowledged the possibility while urging voters to support candidates who could ensure greater accountability in the state legislative assembly. He said voters should use the state election to choose representatives who could benefit Johor while ensuring the government remained accountable.

items.title: Penang to get two dog-friendly parks as councils embrace responsible pet ownership
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 8 — Penang is set to have two dog-friendly parks as authorities and animal welfare groups move to create safer spaces for pets while promoting responsible ownership. SPCA shelter manager and treasurer Richard Ong said the 0.2ha facility, estimated to cost about RM15,000, would feature artificial turf, improved drainage, pet play equipment, gazebos and a swimming pool for dogs. Meanwhile, on the island, Penang Island City Council (MBPP) is considering designating Casa Permai Central Park in Tanjung Bungah as the state’s first recognised pet-friendly public park.

items.title: Selangor Sultan does not consent to Friday prayers at shopping malls
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 8 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has not granted consent for surau and musolla in shopping malls across the state to hold Friday prayers for now, the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) said. He said such a move could lead to a decline in the number of congregants at existing mosques and surau authorised to hold Friday prayers, as Muslims would have the option to perform the prayers at surau or musolla in shopping malls. “At present, there are 448 mosques and 379 surau in Selangor that have been authorised to conduct Friday prayers.

items.title: BN does not deserve second chance over Johor development failures, says Lim Guan Eng
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 8 — DAP chairman Lim Guan Eng has claimed that Barisan Nasional (BN) does not deserve a second chance to govern Johor, alleging the coalition failed to ensure balanced development and economic growth that benefits ordinary Johoreans. Speaking at a ceramah in support of Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Penggaram state seat Felicia Poh in Taman Bukit Pasir here yesterday, the Bagan MP said voters should assess whether economic progress in the state had translated into a better quality of life for them. Lim claimed many Johoreans, particularly those outside the southern region, had not benefited equally from the state’s economic growth.

items.title: Perak’s TVET elderly care programme projected to generate RM1.75m income for practitioners
items.summary: IPOH, July 8 — The Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Elderly Care Programme implemented by the Perak state government since 2023 is projected to generate RM1.75 million in cumulative income for its practitioners, driven by growing demand for elderly care services. Salbiah said about 66 per cent of those trained were now directly involved in providing elderly care services, while others applied their skills to care for family members. She said some participants had also turned their expertise into social enterprises by establishing businesses in the elderly care sector.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued for parts of peninsular Malaysia, Sarawak and Labuan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several areas in Peninsular Malaysia, Sarawak and the Federal Territory of Labuan until noon today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected in several areas, including Kuala Muda, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah, as well as the whole of Penang. Labuan is also expected to experience thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds during the period.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: MPs to raise LPT3 plans, crackdown on harmful vape products
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 8 — The proposed implementation of the East Coast Expressway Phase 3 (LPT3) through a public-private partnership (PPP) model and measures to curb syndicates distributing harmful vape products targeting youths and school students are among the focus of the Dewan Rakyat sitting today. According to the Order Paper on the Parliament’s official website, during Question Time, Wan Hassan Mohd Ramli (PN-Dungun) will ask the Works Minister to state the government’s key considerations for the proposed implementation of LPT3 via the PPP model, including its implications for toll rates and the project’s implementation timeline. Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin (PN-Masjid Tanah) will ask the Home Minister to explain the stricter enforcement measures planned to curb syndicates distributing harmful vape products targeting youths and school students.

items.title: Kedah student falls from third floor while trying to catch pigeon in ceiling space
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 8 — The Kedah State Education Department (JPN Kedah) has confirmed an incident yesterday in which a Year Five pupil fell from the third floor of a school in the Padang Terap district. In a statement, the Kedah JPN said the incident occurred during the afternoon session of the Quran and Fardu Ain (KAFA) class. The incident took place before the class began, when the pupil climbed alone into the ceiling space on that floor.

items.title: Police uncover suspected illegal e-waste facility in Bukit Tengah, 11 foreigners held
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 8 — Police raided a premises in Lorong Pengkalan Indah, Bukit Tengah, yesterday and uncovered a suspected illegal electronic waste (e-waste) storage and processing facility. Acting Seberang Perai Tengah district police chief Supt Norazizi Saad said authorities seized a large quantity of e-waste estimated to be worth millions of ringgit and detained 11 foreigners to assist in the investigation during the integrated operation, dubbed Ops Hazard (E-Waste). Norazizi said MBSP issued two compounds against the premises during the operation for operating without a valid licence.

items.title: NGO urges whole-school safeguarding approach after school stabbing in Banting
items.summary: “To strengthen the child protection ecosystem in schools, BJCK proposes that every school have a comprehensive Child Protection Policy that is consistently implemented, with clear procedures for prevention, reporting, risk management, and intervention,” it said in a statement yesterday. BJCK said other measures should include appointing a designated officer or team responsible for protection and safety in every school to serve as a focal point for coordinating child protection issues, managing complaints and overseeing follow-up action. It also said school management should strengthen regular training for administrators, teachers, and support staff on child protection, early detection of risk factors, trauma-informed practice, and conflict management.
